remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an herbal decoction using Asparagus racemosus that may help with premature ejaculation, start by gathering the dried root or rhizome of the plant, also known as Shatavari. Begin by boiling 1-2 teaspoons of the dried root in 1 cup of water for about 5-7 minutes. The water level should be above the herbs to allow for easy simmering. Reduce the heat to low and let it cook for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their active compounds into the water. As the decoction cooks, the water will start to reduce, so you can add a little more water if needed. Once the decoction has finished cooking, let it cool, then strain the liquid to remove the herbs. You can store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. To use, drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day. intro_desc: Premature ejaculation is a common issue that affects many men, causing stress and impacting relationships. It's essential to address this problem because it can lead to low self-esteem, anxiety, and even depression. To help alleviate this issue, herbal decoctions can be a natural and effective solution. Two herbs that have been shown to be beneficial are Panax ginseng and Ginkgo biloba.  Panax ginseng, also known as Asian ginseng, is an adaptogenic herb that helps the body adapt to stress. It increases the production of nitric oxide, which improves blood flow to the penis, allowing for better erections and longer-lasting sex. Ginseng also contains compounds that help reduce stress and anxiety, making it an excellent herb for men with premature ejaculation.  Ginkgo biloba, on the other hand, is an herb that improves blood flow to the brain and other parts of the body. It contains flavonoids and terpenoids that help relax blood vessels, allowing for better circulation and reducing stress. Ginkgo biloba also has a neuroprotective effect, which helps improve cognitive function and memory, making it an excellent herb for men with premature ejaculation.  Herbal decoctions are a great way to incorporate these herbs into your daily routine. By making a tea or infusion with Panax ginseng and Ginkgo biloba, you can reap the benefits of these herbs and potentially improve your sex life. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications. By addressing premature ejaculation with herbal decoctions, you can improve your self-esteem, reduce stress and anxiety, and enjoy a healthier and more fulfilling sex life. Regular sex can also improve your overall physical health, boost your mood, and even reduce the risk of certain health conditions like heart disease and diabetes.

supplementary_avoid: If you have premature ejaculation, you should avoid using herbal decoctions that may stimulate the nervous system or increase heart rate. Yerba mate and Guayusa are both stimulants that can speed up the heart rate, making it harder to control ejaculation. They contain caffeine, which can make you feel more alert and anxious, leading to premature ejaculation. Yohimbe is another herb to avoid, as it can increase blood pressure and heart rate, making it more difficult to control ejaculation. It can also cause anxiety and jitteriness, making it harder to relax and last longer during sex. Similarly, Kola nut and Ephedra can also increase heart rate and stimulate the nervous system, making them unsuitable for people with premature ejaculation. These herbs may worsen the condition and make it harder to control ejaculation.

Finally, some people find that changing their diet and avoiding certain foods can help with premature ejaculation.
